perfect_label.png._notes: STONE'S THROW IPA — clean, straight, all fields correct. Baseline happy path. Exercises brand, class, ABV, net contents, producer, and government warning.

expected.producer: Stone's Throw Brewing Co., Little Rock, AR

angled_glare.png._notes: Same content as perfect_label but rotated ~12 degrees. Tests OpenCV deskew + OCR robustness to rotation.

expected.producer: Stone's Throw Brewing Co., Little Rock, AR

warning_violation_titlecase.png._notes: Warning header reads 'Government Warning:' in title case — this is the violation. Body otherwise correct. Expected verdict: REVIEW (only the warning fails the strict caps check).

expected.producer: Stone's Throw Brewing Co., Little Rock, AR

brand_caps_mismatch.png._notes: Label brand prints as 'Stone's Throw' (title case); application form has 'STONE'S THROW' (all caps) — Dave's example. Fuzzy matcher should still match.

expected.producer: Stone's Throw Brewing Co., Little Rock, AR

low_contrast.png._notes: STONE'S THROW IPA in very light gray on off-white paper. Tests CLAHE preprocessing. With programmatic labels the text is still readable, but contrast is notably reduced.

stylized_font.png._notes: Dark background, gold text, smaller font sizes. Tests OCR on inverted contrast and reduced text size.

imported_wine.png._notes: CHATEAU DE LA PAIX, Red Wine, 13.5%. Label renders '90 mL' (deliberate mismatch — form says 750 mL). No government warning on this label (simulates front-label-only photo). Expected verdict: REJECT (net contents mismatch + missing warning).

missing_warning.png._notes: LOCAL CRAFT BEER — no GOVERNMENT WARNING block at all. Defining test for the 'required field absent' detection. Expected verdict: REJECT.
